data.narrative:
Neros, a Series B-stage Defense & Security company founded by Soren Monroe-Anderson and Olaf Hichwa, has raised $121M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public entities (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $121M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $230M. The peer-median method benchmarks Neros against six same-stage, same-sector comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B; the cohort median is $1.2B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the Defense & Security peer set, likely driven by differences in revenue scale, IP defensibility, or regulatory pathway maturity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $195M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $715M. The two-order-of-magnitude spread between methods reflects fundamental uncertainty regarding Neros's relative competitive standing and revenue trajectory versus peers. Resolution hinges on specifics: near-term revenue run rate, customer concentration, margin profile, and go-to-market velocity relative to the Vannevar Labs and Dream Security cohort. A midpoint around $700M–$800M may better reflect Series B stage dynamics absent additional performance data.
